voice.diction: Precise, methodical English solicitor's clerk; keeps a shorthand diary; formal, observant, understated.
voice.temperament: Rational and dutiful, unnerved into dread as reason fails him; devoted to Mina.
voice.speech_sample: I must keep writing at every chance, for I dare not stop to think of the horror of it.
voice.worldview: A man of business abroad on a client's errand, slowly realizing his host is not a man.

voice.diction: Archaic, courtly, imperious; long cadences; speaks of his ancient bloodline with pride.
voice.temperament: Gracious host masking predatory menace; patient, proud, controlling.
voice.speech_sample: Welcome to my house! Enter freely and of your own will.
voice.worldview: An ancient warlord-noble who intends to move among the teeming millions of London.

voice.diction: Warm, intelligent, orderly; a schoolmistress who keeps a journal and learns shorthand and typewriting.
voice.temperament: Steadfast, tender, brave; the moral centre; anxious for Jonathan and Lucy.
voice.speech_sample: I feel so happy tonight, and yet I cannot help a shadow of fear.
voice.worldview: A capable, loving woman worried for her absent Jonathan and her dear friend Lucy.

voice.diction: Playful, affectionate, girlish; confides freely to Mina.
voice.temperament: Sweet, flirtatious, guileless; given to sleepwalking; increasingly frail.
voice.speech_sample: Why can't they let a girl marry three men, or as many as want her, and save all this trouble?
voice.worldview: A happy young woman newly engaged, untroubled — until strange dreams begin.

voice.diction: Clinical, precise; a physician who dictates his diary to a phonograph; scientific scepticism.
voice.temperament: Earnest, disciplined, quietly heartbroken over Lucy; loyal to Van Helsing.
voice.speech_sample: I must be careful not to let a fixed idea run away with me; the facts are strange enough.
voice.worldview: A young alienist running an asylum, puzzled by a patient and by an illness he cannot cure.

voice.temperament: Brave and loving; devastated by Lucy's decline; steadfast in the hunt.
voice.speech_sample: Tell me what I am to do, and I shall not falter.
voice.worldview: A young lord newly engaged to Lucy, ready to give his very blood for her.

voice.diction: Frank, warm American idiom; frontier plainness and courage.
voice.temperament: Gallant, generous, fearless; a good loser in love and a staunch friend.
voice.speech_sample: Miss Lucy, I know I ain't good enough to regulate the fixin's of your little shoes.
voice.worldview: An adventuring Texan who loves Lucy and stands by his friends to the last.

voice.diction: Alternately lucid and raving; fixated, grandiose; speaks of lives and blood.
voice.temperament: A zoophagous madman craving life; cunning, servile to a coming master.
voice.speech_sample: The blood is the life! The blood is the life!
voice.worldview: An asylum patient who consumes small lives, sensing a great one approaching across the sea.

voice.diction: Dutch-accented, tumbling English; aphoristic, learned, digressive; mixes science and old faith.
voice.temperament: Brilliant, kindly, iron-willed; a metaphysician of open mind who names the unnameable.
voice.speech_sample: There are mysteries which men can only guess at, which age by age they may solve only in part.
voice.worldview: A philosopher-physician who knows what modern science refuses to believe, come to fight it.

voice.diction: Low, sweet, wicked; musical and predatory.
voice.temperament: Voluptuous and merciless brides of the Count.
voice.speech_sample: He is young and strong; there are kisses for us all.
voice.worldview: The Count's undead brides, kept in the castle, hungering for the living.

facts.subject: Jonathan Harker
facts.text: Jonathan Harker travels through Transylvania to Castle Dracula on solicitor's business.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: Count Dracula welcomes Jonathan into his castle as his guest.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: The Count is arranging to purchase the estate of Carfax at Purfleet near London.

facts.subject: Jonathan Harker
facts.text: Jonathan realizes he is a prisoner in the castle.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: The Count casts no reflection in a mirror.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: The Count crawls face-down down the sheer castle wall like a lizard.

facts.subject: The Weird Sisters
facts.text: Three vampire women in the castle try to feed on Jonathan until the Count stops them.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: Jonathan finds the Count lying gorged with blood in a box of earth by day.

facts.subject: Jonathan Harker
facts.text: Jonathan resolves to escape the castle by climbing the wall.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Lucy receives three proposals in one day, from Seward, Quincey Morris, and Arthur Holmwood.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Lucy accepts Arthur Holmwood's proposal of marriage.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: Mina Murray is engaged to Jonathan Harker and anxious at his long silence.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: Mina and Lucy stay at Whitby, where Lucy resumes sleepwalking.

facts.subject: Renfield
facts.text: Seward studies Renfield, a patient who eats flies and spiders and says the blood is the life.

facts.subject: The Demeter
facts.text: A storm drives the derelict schooner Demeter aground at Whitby with her dead captain lashed to the wheel.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: The Demeter's cargo of earth-boxes carries the Count in secret to England.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Mina finds Lucy sleepwalking at night with a dark figure bending over her.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Two small puncture wounds appear on Lucy's throat and she grows pale and languid.

facts.subject: Jonathan Harker
facts.text: Mina is summoned to Buda-Pesth where Jonathan is recovering, and they marry.

facts.subject: Abraham Van Helsing
facts.text: Seward calls in his old master Professor Van Helsing to examine the failing Lucy.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Van Helsing orders blood transfusions and Arthur is the first to give his blood to Lucy.

facts.subject: Abraham Van Helsing
facts.text: Van Helsing hangs garlic flowers about Lucy's room without explaining why.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: A wolf breaks Lucy's window, the garland is disturbed, and she sinks toward death.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Lucy Westenra dies despite the transfusions and the garlic.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: A strange change comes over Lucy's face at the moment of death, her teeth sharper.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: News spreads of a 'bloofer lady' luring children on Hampstead Heath and leaving them with throat wounds.

facts.subject: Abraham Van Helsing
facts.text: Van Helsing tells Seward that Lucy is Un-Dead and that a vampire is at work.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Lucy has become a vampire and rises from her tomb by night to prey on children.

facts.subject: Lucy Westenra
facts.text: Arthur drives a stake through the vampire Lucy's heart to free her soul.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: Mina joins the group and collates every diary and letter into one typed record.

facts.subject: The Crew of Light
facts.text: The hunters vow together to destroy Count Dracula.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: The hunters sterilize the Count's boxes of earth with holy wafer, one by one.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: Dracula forces Mina to drink his blood, binding her to him.

facts.subject: Renfield
facts.text: Renfield is fatally injured after defying the Count who used him.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: A touched Host wafer scars Mina's forehead, marking her as unclean.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: Dracula flees England by ship toward his castle in Transylvania.

facts.subject: Mina Murray
facts.text: Van Helsing hypnotizes Mina to track the Count's location through her bond with him.

facts.subject: The Crew of Light
facts.text: The hunters pursue Dracula's earth-box across Europe toward the castle.

facts.subject: Quincey Morris
facts.text: Quincey Morris is killed in the final fight but lives to see Mina freed.

facts.subject: Count Dracula
facts.text: Count Dracula is destroyed at his castle and crumbles to dust as Mina's scar fades.
